column7: message

Killing an animal does not have to cause it suffering. 

Nearly all meat production today, however, does causes extreme levels of suffering; not by the killing itself, but by the way they live in the time before. This I would agree is not moral to support. 

However, there are a handful of meat options in which the relationship between predator and prey is just that, and you could not be called any more immoral than a bear. 

In practical terms, you're probably right, but in theoretical terms, it is not the meat consumption itself that is immoral.

Okay, maybe the title should have been 'It's morally wrong to facilitate the murder of animals', but I guess it's just not as snappy ;)
The 'Killing an animal does not necessarily cause suffering' is an interesting stance, but not one I agree with. If you're doing it quickly enough, you could argue this but I see it as a fairly philosophical thing at that point. Not to mention that it's entirely plausible that animals have actual wants and needs and motives that don't involve being killed, so I'd really want to go on the side of caution, that doesn't involve them being killed on a huge scale.

I understand where both of you are coming from, but let's be honest, the reality of factory farming is far from ideal. It’s not just about the moment of death, but the entire life these animals lead. They deserve better. We should focus on reducing harm overall, not just debating theoretical morality.

column7: @descriptive-cone I hear you, and I agree that the conditions in factory farms are terrible. But my point is more about the act of killing itself. Even under humane conditions, I still think we should question why we need to kill animals on such a massive scale. It’s not just about their last moments, but the principle behind it all.

column7:
> But, really, you don't think someone can consider themselves liberal if they don't like the things someone else voted for?

That isn't what I said. I said you shouldn't be angry at them. 

>What if someone voted to put all non-Christians to death. Should a liberal be OK with that?

No you shouldn't be okay with that. Obviously not. You're strawmanning my argument.

My argument is obviously in the context of a reasonable democracy.

And, In the context of trump, the amount of self proclaimed liberals I've seen who have completely dropped the ball regarding Trump supporters has been astounding.

That's ridiculous.  I'd argue you wouldn't be liberal if you didn't think the person should be allowed to have the liberty to vote for who they thought was best.  But, really, you don't think someone can consider themselves liberal if they don't like the things someone else voted for?  What if someone voted to put all non-Christians to death.  Should a liberal be OK with that?

@teal-histogram I get where you're coming from but it's not just about the right to vote. It's about the consequences of those votes. If someone supports policies that harm others, can they still call themselves liberal? Just saying.

column7:
Safe to say OP is trying to find a way to call people racists... and lump a nitwit in with a man who spent his entire life, and lost his life, in the pursuit of justice.

This is a false dilemma that says “if you don’t like CK, then you don’t like MLK” ... different times, different problems, different quality of character between the two.

It is disgustingly subjective (as OP admits in his opening sentence).  

CMV: if you hate Bionicles, then *ahem* it’s safe to say you would also hate legos.
@user mashed-sword I think the point is about drawing fair comparisons. Comparing someone like MLK to CK isn't helpful. Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the actual issues.

column7:
>So, one of the big points we are taught when it comes to taxation is that it is necesary for a society to function, a country can not rely solely on it's industry and exports to mantain a good level of life for its citizens.

I could think of any number of scenarios where I would rely on theft to survive.  But it's still theft.
column19: ['Active', 'Democrat', 'Takes work calls at any time of the day']

column7:
> Calling it theft completely misses the point of taxation

No it doesn't, because calling it theft is not addressing the utility of said theft one way or the other.

Guys, let’s keep it civil. Taxation isn’t theft; it’s how we fund public services and infrastructure that benefit everyone. Without it, we wouldn’t have roads, schools, or emergency services. Let’s focus on constructive dialogue.

column7:
>why are there white people that are so intent on being able to use the n-word just like black people do? 

Because the word exists and there is utility in being able to say it in certain contexts, regardless of who you are.

>places and times but suddenly a word white people invented to dehumanize black people is reclaimed by black people

Sorry, but I don't see the use in trying to reclaim a word. A very well-known word isn't an object that a particular group of people can keep in its possession. You're just not likely to succeed in barring millions of people from using it and simultaneously keep relations between its users and non-users harmonious.

> and white people get so interested in the nature and philosophy of words, but only when it comes to his particular word for some reason.

I'm actually interested in the philosophy of language and in fact study it as part of my degree, but it's got nothing to do with my annoyance at people trying to bar me from saying nigger lol.

> All white people should probably just fucking drop this already. It’s so meaningless, it’s so insignificant

Wrongly policing someone's language is not insignificant.

> All the time in English words are relegated to certain people

> we do that all the fucking time with other groups. That’s pathetic.

I'd like to know what are the other words that are relegated to other people, and where in those cases it is morally right to do so?

Why does it matter? Fuck everything else you said, but *why* are there white people that are so *intent* on being able to use the n-word just like black people do? And why do black people react harshly against the word even when used in “innocuous” ways?

You want to know why? Because the black man can’t have shit for himself. All the time in English words are relegated to certain people, places and times but suddenly a word white people invented to dehumanize black people is reclaimed by black people and white people get so interested in the nature and philosophy of words, but only when it comes to this particular word for some reason. 

All white people should probably just fucking drop this already. It’s so meaningless, it’s so insignificant, and all it does is take something inherently, unarguably rooted in black culture and try to destroy the value it holds there.

In short: you shouldn’t give a shit. Entitled ass white people can’t just let black America have a word when we do that *all the fucking time* with other groups. That’s pathetic.
